Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:34 ET
P/C divergence at multi-month extreme as institutions hedge dispersion risk
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts vs single-name calls divergence (1.34 vs 0.51) signals institutional dispersion hedging ahead of GDP print
  • 2. Positive gamma environment (+$226.6M SPY GEX) creates dampened volatility despite elevated put hedging demand
  • 3. Six names showing vol/skew divergences indicate single-stock inefficiencies amid correlation breakdown
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge positioning

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between index puts (1.34) and single-name calls (0.51) — among the widest spreads since August's unwind. This dispersion hedge pattern reflects institutions protecting broad portfolios while maintaining individual stock upside exposure, consistent with the 0.03 implied correlation reading that indicates stock-specific regime conditions. The massive SPY put blocks (200k+ June 530P, 118k+ April 710P) confirm defensive positioning into Thursday's GDP print, even as single-name call flow in NVDA (159k+ calls across strikes) and TSLA (78k+ calls) maintains bullish conviction. Dealer positioning provides crucial context for interpreting this defensive flow — SPY's +$226.6M positive gamma means these put purchases are being absorbed by dealers who will dampen any resulting volatility. The gamma flip sits just 30 cents below current levels at $708.22, creating a narrow band where dealer hedging behavior could shift from stabilizing to amplifying. This positive gamma cushion explains why VIX remains contained at 19.0 despite the put-heavy flow, though backwardation in 15 names including AMD and ARM suggests near-term event risk is being priced aggressively. The vol divergence flags highlight specific inefficiencies worth monitoring — six names showing vol high/skew flat patterns (IBIT, EEM, KWEB) indicate single-stock opportunities, while the rich vol/call heavy signals in defensive names like PG and WMT suggest institutions are paying up for protection in traditionally safe sectors.

Layer 1: Sentiment +18Layer 2: GEX +$226.6MLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.34/0.51

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
